description: Neil and Chris. West End Girls. Synth-pop intellectuals who never blinked.

name: Petula Clark
description: Downtown. The British voice that conquered American radio in '64.

name: Pixies
description: Loud quiet loud. Black Francis screaming, Kim Deal holding it down. Where Is My Mind started a revolution.

name: Placebo
description: Brian Molko's nasal sneer. Pure Morning. Glam goth for the Y2K generation.

name: R.E.M.
description: Athens, Georgia. Murmur to Automatic. Invented college rock, then outgrew it. Losing My Religion.

name: RBD
description: Rebelde. Six Mexican kids from a telenovela who became the biggest Latin pop group of the 2000s. Ser o Parecer. Solo Quedate en Silencio. Estadio Azteca, sold out.

name: Red Hot Chili Peppers
description: californication all around the world, its all love

name: Rick Nelson
description: Hello Mary Lou. Travelin' Man. Teen idol who actually played guitar and meant it. Garden Party is the greatest I don't care what you think song ever recorded.

name: Rick Springfield
description: Jessie's Girl. Australian soap actor turned power-pop hit machine.

name: Rihanna
description: Umbrella to ANTI. Barbados to the top of everything. Eight albums in eight years, each one a different Rihanna.
eric_take: rihanna makes hits left and right holy shit

name: Robbie Williams
description: Take That's wildcard gone solo. Angels is the biggest pub singalong in British history. Feel is pure pop perfection. Owned the UK like nobody since.

name: Sarah Vaughan
description: The Divine One. A voice that could bend notes into shapes nobody else could find. Lullaby of Birdland is jazz vocals at the peak of the mountain.

name: Simple Minds
description: Glasgow post-punk that grew into something cinematic. Jim Kerr filling stadiums. The Breakfast Club made everyone forget how dark the early work was.

name: SMAP
description: Five guys, fifteen million records, a televised farewell that broke a nation. Sekai ni Hitotsu Dake no Hana.

name: Snoop Dogg
description: Gin and Juice. That drawl. Doggystyle dropped and the West Coast never sounded the same. From Long Beach to everything, forever.

name: Steps
description: Tragedy. Five Brits in matching outfits, choreographed to within an inch of their lives.

name: Supergrass
description: Caught by the Fuzz at 17. Alright is the sound of being young and not caring. Pumping on Your Stereo. Britpop's most underrated band, full stop.

name: Suzi Quatro
description: The first female bass player to become a rock star. 48 Crash. Can the Can. Leather jumpsuit, no apologies. Joan Jett took notes.

name: Sweet
description: Ballroom Blitz. Glam rock's snarl and sparkle.

name: Talk Talk
description: Started synth pop, ended post-rock. Spirit of Eden is the most important left turn in music history. Mark Hollis whispered and the world leaned in.

name: The Drifters
description: Under the Boardwalk. Save the Last Dance for Me. Up on the Roof. They wrote the soundtrack to every summer you wish you had.

name: The Everly Brothers
description: Don and Phil's harmonies. Bye Bye Love. The blueprint for every duo since.

name: The Jacksons
description: Five brothers from Gary, Indiana. Then six. Michael's launchpad and Jermaine's home.

name: The Jimi Hendrix Experience
description: Three albums. That's all it took. Purple Haze. Voodoo Child. All Along the Watchtower. The greatest guitarist who ever lived, gone at 27.

name: The Smiths
description: Morrissey's misery, Marr's chiming guitars. How Soon Is Now? Forever.

name: Thievery Corporation
description: DC's own downtempo ambassadors. Lebanese Blonde. The Mirror Conspiracy at 2 AM with the lights low. Dub, bossa nova, trip-hop — no borders, no genre, just vibes.

name: Tom Jones
description: It's Not Unusual. Welsh roar. Knickers on stage. Sex Bomb at 60.

name: Tommy Roe
description: Sheila. Dizzy. Bubblegum pop's sugar dealer.

name: Tool
description: Lateralus. Fibonacci spirals and seven-minute time signatures. Maynard screams and you feel it in your spine. Thirteen years between albums and nobody stopped waiting.

name: Van Halen
description: Eddie's tapping. Diamond Dave's split. Eruption changed what a guitar could do.

name: Vanessa Hudgens
description: Say OK. Come Back to Me. High School Musical's Gabriella stepped out solo and the V album had more edge than anyone expected.

name: Waka Flocka Flame
description: Hard in da Paint. No Hands. Flockaveli is the purest distillation of go-dumb energy ever recorded.
eric_take: in the mood for some ignorant waka flocka shit

name: Westlife
description: Five Irish lads on stools. Flying Without Wings. Boyband ballad factory.

name: Whigfield
description: Saturday Night. The dance that owned 1994. Danish voice over Italian Eurodance production.

name: 嵐
description: Five-piece Johnny's storm. A.RA.SHI. Two decades of J-pop dominance before the indefinite hiatus.

name: 松田聖子
description: Showa-era idol queen. Akai Sweet Pea. Defined what kawaii meant on stage.

name: Bad Bunny
description: El Conejo Malo. Reggaeton to experimental. Puerto Rico's biggest global export.

name: 21 Savage
description: British passport, Atlanta trap. Savage Mode II with Metro Boomin is the blueprint. A lot is an understatement.

name: Adele
description: Named her albums after her age. 21 broke every record that existed. Rolling in the Deep is fury disguised as pop.

name: Alvvays
description: Canadian dream pop perfection. Archie, Marry Me is the hook that never leaves. Two v's, zero filler.

name: Arctic Monkeys
description: I Bet You Look Good on the Dancefloor. Sheffield lads to lounge lizards. Alex Turner reinvents himself every album.

name: ATEEZ
description: K-pop with pirate energy. Eight members, zero chill. Guerrilla and Wonderland go harder than they need to.

name: Bauhaus
description: Bela Lugosi's Dead. Nine minutes of gothic post-punk that invented an entire subculture. Peter Murphy's cheekbones did the rest.

name: black midi
description: London math rock chaos. Schlagenheim sounds like a car crash you can't stop replaying. Geordie Greep screams through jazz.

name: Boston
description: More Than a Feeling. Tom Scholz built a spaceship in his basement. The debut sold 17 million copies.

name: boygenius
description: Phoebe, Julien, Lucy. Three singer-songwriters who didn't need a supergroup but gave us one anyway.

name: Buddy Holly
description: Died at 22. Peggy Sue, That'll Be the Day, Everyday. Invented the rock band format. The plane crash that stopped the music.

name: Captain Beefheart
description: Don Van Vliet. Trout Mask Replica is either the greatest album ever made or unlistenable noise. No middle ground.

name: Caroline Polachek
description: Ex-Chairlift, solo siren. Bunny Is a Rider is hyperpop art school. That voice bends in ways voices shouldn't.

name: Central Cee
description: West London drill gone global. Doja is the crossover. UK rap's most exportable voice.

name: Chappell Roan
description: Midwest princess. Good Luck, Babe! is the queer pop anthem of the decade. Zero to stadiums in one album cycle.

name: Chicago
description: Jazz-rock with horns. 25 or 6 to 4 is the riff. Saturday in the Park is the hit. Then Peter Cetera turned them into a ballad machine.

name: Childish Gambino
description: Donald Glover rapping, singing, directing, writing. Because the Internet to Awaken My Love. Constant reinvention.
eric_take: i feel dirty for liking this childish gambino album

name: Dead Kennedys
description: Jello Biafra's satire was sharper than most punk's anger. Holiday in Cambodia. California Uber Alles. West Coast hardcore with a brain.

name: Destiny’s Child
description: Beyoncé's origin story. Say My Name. Bills, Bills, Bills. Survivor. Kelly and Michelle held their own. The group that launched a dynasty.

name: Dire Straits
description: Mark Knopfler's fingers. Sultans of Swing is the audition tape. Money for Nothing is the MTV anthem. Brothers in Arms sold 30 million.

name: Doja Cat
description: Mooo! was a meme. Say So was a TikTok smash. Planet Her proved she's an actual artist. Scarlet proved she doesn't care what you think.

name: Donovan
description: Britain's answer to Dylan. Sunshine Superman, Mellow Yellow, Hurdy Gurdy Man. Psychedelic folk before that was a genre.

name: Ed Sheeran
description: Loop pedal busker to biggest pop star on Earth. Shape of You. Thinking Out Loud. The math symbols keep coming.

name: Eurythmics
description: Sweet Dreams Are Made of This. Annie Lennox's voice over Dave Stewart's synths. Who am I to disagree.

name: Feid
description: Ferxxo. Medellín's reggaeton prince. FERXXOCALIPSIS is the party. Collaboration machine with Karol G and everyone else.

name: Fontaines D.C.
description: Dublin post-punk. Dogrel is poetry shouted over guitars. Boys in the Better Land is the anthem. Ireland's loudest export.

name: Franz Ferdinand
description: Take Me Out. That guitar riff change is the moment indie disco was born. Scottish art school groove.

name: Fugees
description: The Score. Lauryn Hill, Wyclef, Pras. Killing Me Softly reimagined. Ready or Not. One perfect album then implosion.

name: Ghost
description: Swedish metal dressed as satanic pop. Square Hammer is an arena anthem. Tobias Forge is the only constant behind the mask.

name: Gojira
description: French environmental metal. Flying Whales is the song. From Mars to Sirius is the album. Heavy as tectonic plates.

name: Grace Jones
description: Jamaican-American icon. Nightclubbing is art. Pull Up to the Bumper is funk. Slave to the Rhythm is eternal. Fashion, model, force of nature.

name: Grimes
description: Claire Boucher built Visions on GarageBand in two weeks. Oblivion is the song. Art Angels is the pop pivot. Cyberpunk before it was cringe.

name: Hozier
description: Take Me to Church. Irish folk-soul with a baritone that fills cathedrals. Too Sweet broke streaming records in 2024.

name: Ice Spice
description: Bronx drill princess. Munch was the moment. Y2K is the debut. Gen Z energy distilled into ad-libs.

name: Jack Harlow
description: Louisville charm. First Class sampled Fergie and went number one. Industry Baby with Lil Nas X. Frat rap grown up.

name: Janis Joplin
description: Me and Bobby McGee. Piece of My Heart. The voice that tore itself apart. Pearl came out posthumous. 27 forever.

name: Japanese Breakfast
description: Michelle Zauner turned grief into Psychopomp. Jubilee is joy after mourning. Also wrote Crying in H Mart. Polymath.

name: Juan Gabriel
description: El Divo de Juárez. Querida. Amor Eterno. Mexico's greatest songwriter. 42 albums. Performed for millions. Cried with all of them.

name: KAROL G
description: La Bichota. TQG with Shakira was the crossover. Mañana Será Bonito is the album. Colombia to the world.

name: Knocked Loose
description: Kentucky hardcore. Counting Worms is violence. You Won't Go Before You're Supposed To proved heavy music still moves.

name: Leonard Cohen
description: Hallelujah. Suzanne. Bird on the Wire. Canadian poet who sang in a baritone whisper. Wrote until the very end.

name: Lil Peep
description: Emo rap pioneer. Awful Things. Star Shopping. Gone at 21. Opened a door between punk and SoundCloud rap that never closed.

name: Magdalena Bay
description: Synth-pop duo. Mercurial World is a simulation. Imaginal Disk is the upgrade. Retro-futurism with a wink.

name: Maroon 5
description: Songs About Jane is a great debut. This Love, She Will Be Loved. Then Adam Levine became a TV judge and the band became a hit factory.

name: Mastodon
description: Progressive sludge metal. Leviathan is Moby Dick as a concept album. Crack the Skye goes to space. Blood Mountain splits the difference.

name: Megan Thee Stallion
description: Hot Girl Summer. Savage remix with Beyoncé. Houston rap with a degree. Body-ody-ody.

name: Melanie Martinez
description: Cry Baby is a concept album about childhood trauma dressed in pastels. PORTALS goes alien. Directed her own film. Auteur pop.

name: MGMT
description: Kids. Time to Pretend. Electric Feel. Oracular Spectacular was the blog-era bible. Then they made three deliberately weird albums.

name: Missy Elliott
description: Get Ur Freak On. Work It. Timbaland beats and Missy's vision. Most innovative woman in hip hop history. The rain, supa dupa fly.

name: Mumford & Sons
description: The Cave. Little Lion Man. Banjo folk-rock that filled arenas. Sigh No More started a whole genre wave.

name: Nick Cave
description: The Bad Seeds. Red Right Hand. Into My Arms. Australian gothic. Murder ballads and love songs from the same dark pen.

name: No Doubt
description: Gwen Stefani before solo. Don't Speak. Tragic Kingdom. Ska-punk meets new wave. Just a Girl was the anthem.

name: Olivia Rodrigo
description: Drivers License broke the internet. SOUR is a breakup album that sold 15 million. GUTS proved it wasn't a fluke.

name: One Direction
description: What Makes You Beautiful. Five lads from X Factor. Biggest boy band since the Beatles comparisons. Then Harry went solo.

name: OneRepublic
description: Apologize with Timbaland. Counting Stars. Ryan Tedder writes hits for everyone else too. Pop-rock for the streaming era.

name: Panic! at the Disco
description: I Write Sins Not Tragedies. Brendon Urie's voice and theatrical excess. A Fever You Can't Sweat Out is emo-pop perfection.

name: Patti Smith
description: Horses. Gloria reimagined. Punk poet laureate. Because the Night with Springsteen. Merged poetry and rock before anyone else.

name: Peso Pluma
description: Génesis blew up corridos tumbados worldwide. Ella Baila Sola was inescapable. Mexico's new generation in one voice.

name: Pharrell Williams
description: Half of The Neptunes. Produced half your playlist. Happy became inescapable but G I R L is the real album.

name: Phil Collins
description: In the Air Tonight. That drum fill. Genesis drummer gone solo megastar. Against All Odds. Sussudio. Owned the 80s.

name: PinkPantheress
description: 2-minute songs. TikTok bedroom pop. Boy's a Liar Pt. 2 with Ice Spice. Jungle and drum'n'bass nostalgia from Gen Z.

name: PJ Harvey
description: Rid of Me. Polly Jean shreds, whispers, screams. Let England Shake won the Mercury Prize. Reinvents herself every album.

name: Portishead
description: Dummy invented trip-hop. Glory Box. Sour Times. Beth Gibbons' voice over scratchy vinyl beats. Third went industrial and disappeared.

name: Post Malone
description: Congratulations. White Iverson. Genre? Yes. Beer pong to billion streams pipeline.

name: Procol Harum
description: A Whiter Shade of Pale. That organ intro is one of the most recognizable in rock. Then 12 more albums nobody talks about.

name: Rauw Alejandro
description: Puerto Rican R&B meets reggaeton. Todo de Ti is the hit. Cosa Nuestra goes full salsa. Latin pop's smoothest operator.

name: Roxy Music
description: Bryan Ferry's sophistication meets Brian Eno's chaos. Avalon is the smoothest album ever made. For Your Pleasure is the weird one.

name: Sabrina Carpenter
description: Espresso. Disney kid to pop star pipeline done right. Short n' Sweet is the summer album. Nonsense outros are the secret weapon.

name: Siouxsie and the Banshees
description: Juju. Spellbound. Hong Kong Garden. Gothic post-punk royalty. Siouxsie Sioux's voice cuts through everything.

name: Slayyyter
description: Hyperpop provocateur. Troubled Paradise is Y2K pop maximalism. Daddy AF. Internet pop with zero filter.

name: Squid
description: Brighton post-punk. Bright Green Field is chaotic and brainy. Narrator is seven minutes of controlled panic.

name: Supertramp
description: Breakfast in America. The Logical Song. Crime of the Century. Art-pop disguised as radio rock. That Wurlitzer sound.

name: Swedish House Mafia
description: Don't You Worry Child. Three DJs who broke EDM into the mainstream. Save the World. One reunion tour at a time.

name: Tems
description: Nigerian Afrobeats meets R&B. Free Mind. Featured on Drake and Future, then Born in the Wild proved she doesn't need features.

name: The Killers
description: Mr. Brightside never left the UK charts. Brandon Flowers brought Las Vegas drama to indie rock.

name: The Mamas & the Papas
description: California Dreamin'. Monday Monday. Laurel Canyon harmonies. Four voices that defined the 60s folk-pop sound.

name: The Offspring
description: Self Esteem. Come Out and Play. Pretty Fly for a White Guy. Smash sold 11 million on Epitaph. 90s punk-pop kings.

name: The Stooges
description: Iggy Pop invented punk before punk existed. I Wanna Be Your Dog. Raw Power. Fun House is a noise masterpiece.

name: The Verve
description: Bitter Sweet Symphony. That string sample. Richard Ashcroft walking down the street. Urban Hymns is Britpop's last great album.

name: Thin Lizzy
description: The Boys Are Back in Town. Phil Lynott. Twin guitar harmonies. Jailbreak is the album. Irish rock's greatest.

name: Tito Puente
description: El Rey del Timbal. Oye Como Va before Santana covered it. Dance Mania. 100+ albums. The king of Latin jazz.

name: TLC
description: Waterfalls. No Scrubs. CrazySexyCool sold 23 million. T-Boz, Left Eye, Chilli. Best-selling girl group in American history.

name: Turnstile
description: Baltimore hardcore meets summer vibes. GLOW ON broke hardcore out of basements. Mystery is the crossover.

name: twenty one pilots
description: Stressed Out. Ride. Heathens. Tyler Joseph and Josh Dun turned anxiety into arena anthems. Blurryface went diamond.

name: Village People
description: Y.M.C.A. Macho Man. Disco camp perfection. Every wedding, every sporting event. They knew exactly what they were doing.

name: Wet Leg
description: Chaise Longue. Isle of Wight duo with deadpan humor and guitar hooks. The debut won a Brit Award. Moisturizer keeps it going.

name: Yeah Yeah Yeahs
description: Maps. Karen O's feral energy. Zero is the glam hit. Fever to Tell is art-punk perfection. NYC's wildest.

name: Yeat
description: Twizzy. Flëw. Autonomous trap. Lyfestyle with Drake. The sound of rap's algorithmic future. Tonka.

name: Young Thug
description: Rewrote the rules of rap melody. So Much Fun is the thesis statement. Then they locked him up.
eric_take: young thug while my grandma talks / you cant full appreciate young thug without lyrics mannn / hang up the phone with grandma turn on the young thug

name: ZAYN
description: Left the biggest band in the world and made it interesting. R&B with edges. KONNAKOL is the latest chapter.

name: Zach Bryan
description: Something in the Orange. Oklahoma poet. DeAnn was recorded in a barracks. Country's most authentic voice in a decade.

name: Zedd
description: Clarity with Foxes. Stay with Alessia Cara. Russian-German producer who brought EDM to pop radio.

name: Flea
description: a plee from flee

name: John Frusciante
description: RHCP's secret weapon, solo catalog deeper than most careers. Niandra LaDes is raw nerve. Curtains is gorgeous. Shadows Collide With People is the masterpiece nobody heard.

name: Ella Langley
description: Hungover. Alabama grit meets Nashville polish.

name: Wiz Khalifa
description: Black and Yellow. Pittsburgh's loudest export turned a Steelers chant into a #1. See You Again broke YouTube and made everyone cry.

name: Metric
description: Help I'm Alive. Toronto synth-rock. Emily Haines's deadpan delivery and James Shaw's hooks — then Black Sheep handed off to Scott Pilgrim and went immortal.
